title: Skydiver Terminal Velocity
body: The terminal velocity of a person falling in air depends upon the weight and the area of the person facing the fluid. An $$80.0-kg$$ skydiver falls in a headfirst position with a surface area of $$0.140$$ $$m^2$$. Take the drag coefficient to be $$0.70$$ and the density of air to be $$1.21$$ $$\frac{kg}{m^3}$$.
